title: crepis aurea subsp. lucida
intro: Crepis aurea subsp. lucida, also known as the bitter crane's-bill or golden hawk's-beard, is a subspecies of the Crepis aurea plant, which is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its bitter and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, reduction of fever, and relief from respiratory problems. The Crepis aurea subsp. lucida plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves, roots, and flowers can be used to create these preparations. Consuming Crepis aurea subsp. lucida in its herbal form has been reported to have additional health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system.
